title: Daily Digest
summary: The Bank of Canada's Daily Digest provides a snapshot of key monetary and financial indicators including exchange rates, money market rates, inflation, interest rates, and bond yields as of March 27-30, 2026. Notable macro data shows CPI inflation fell to 1.8% in February 2026 from 2.3% in January, marking a 0.5 percentage point decline. The Bank's target overnight rate remains at 2.25% with no change from the prior period.
key_points: Bank of Canada target overnight rate maintained at 2.25% as of March 30, 2026
CPI inflation declined to 1.8% in February 2026 from 2.3% in January 2026, a 0.5pp drop
All core inflation measures (CPI-trim, CPI-median, CPI-common) declined month-over-month
USD/CAD exchange rate strengthened to 1.3926 on March 30 from 1.3875 on March 27 (+0.0051)
Government of Canada 10-year benchmark bond yield decreased to 3.83% from 3.88% (-0.05)
Canadian Overnight Repo Rate Average (CORRA) at 2.27% as of March 30
why_it_matters: The significant drop in CPI from 2.3% to 1.8% represents a meaningful decline in Canadian inflation and may have implications for Bank of Canada monetary policy decisions. This data compilation serves as a daily reference for FX traders, fixed income investors, and those monitoring Canadian monetary conditions. The stable overnight rate at 2.25% confirms no policy change, while the declining bond yields suggest easing financial conditions.
macro_context: The 0.5 percentage point drop in Canadian CPI to 1.8% places inflation below the Bank of Canada's 1-3% inflation-control target range midpoint. This deceleration in inflation could be relevant for future rate decisions, though this source is a data compilation and does not include policy commentary or forward guidance.
